info.name: Innerspark API — v2 Endpoints
info.description:
Complete test collection for all endpoints added or modified.

Base URL: http://localhost:8000 (development) | https://server.innersparkafrica.us (production)

Auth: All requests require the `x-api-key` header.

Folders:
1. Support Groups — Directory & Availability
2. Support Groups — Subscriptions
3. Support Groups — Messaging
4. 1-on-1 Chat Sessions — Booking
5. 1-on-1 Chat Sessions — Messaging
6. 1-on-1 Chat Sessions — Presence (Heartbeat)
7. Donations
8. Banners
9. Therapist Slots
10. Notifications
11. Therapist Session Start
12. Therapist List & Session Types

item.name: 1 · Support Groups — Directory & Availability

request.description:
Returns the full list of active support groups, including cohort scheduling info, capacity, subscription price, and the linked therapist's photo and specialisation.

Key response fields:
- `next_cohort_start_date` — ISO-8601 datetime of the next cohort window
- `is_open_for_booking` — true when the cohort starts within 3 days
- `subscription_price_ugx` — cost to join one cohort (default 25,000 UGX)
- `therapistPhoto`, `therapistSpecialization` — therapist profile info

Lightweight pre-payment check. Returns available seats and whether the next cohort is still accepting bookings. The client should call this before initiating the subscribe flow to surface a 'full' message early.

Key response fields:
- `available_seats` — seats remaining before the 10-member cap is hit
- `is_open` — false when the cohort is full or not yet open
- `subscription_price_ugx` — price the user will be charged

Returns the groups the authenticated client has ever subscribed to, including their current membership status and expiry date. Clients use this screen to re-enter an active group chat or see when they can renew.

Key response fields:
- `membership_status` — ACTIVE | EXPIRED | PENDING
- `expiry_date` — when the current subscription window ends
- `last_message` — preview of the most recent group message
- `icon_url`, `therapistImage`, `therapistSpecialization` — display metadata

item.description: Read-only endpoints that allow clients to browse the group catalogue and check cohort availability before subscribing.

item.name: 2 · Support Groups — Subscriptions

request.description:
Pays for and reserves a seat in the next cohort window. Deducts `subscription_price_ugx` from the client's wallet and writes a `PENDING` row to `group_subscription_history`. The subscription becomes `ACTIVE` when the cohort start date is reached.

Validations (in order):
1. `agreeToGuidelines` must be true
2. User must exist and have a verified email
3. Group must be active and open for booking
4. No existing active subscription for this user+group
5. Capacity not yet full (10 members max, row-locked)
6. Wallet balance >= subscription_price_ugx

Passing a user_id that does not exist in the users table must return 401 with 'User not found'. This prevents the fallback 'insufficient balance' error that would otherwise surface when the wallet lookup returns nothing.

exec: // Ensure wallet is empty before this test (run manually via DB or seed script)

request.description: When the user's wallet balance is below the group's subscription_price_ugx, the endpoint returns 400 with error code INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE and a breakdown showing the shortfall amount the user needs to top up.

Extends a lapsed membership by booking the next cohort window (+7 days from next session_day). Deducts subscription_price_ugx from wallet, appends a new row to group_subscription_history (PENDING), and updates group_members.

Prerequisite: user must already be a member (joined via /subscribe at some point) and their current subscription must be expired.
Renewal must be blocked when the user already has an ACTIVE subscription that has not yet expired. This prevents double-charging before the current window is over.

item.description: Endpoints that manage the cohort subscription lifecycle: joining a new cohort, blocking duplicate/active subscriptions, and renewing after expiry.

item.name: 3 · Support Groups — Messaging

request.description:
Returns paginated group messages filtered to only the time windows where the requesting user had an ACTIVE or EXPIRED subscription. This enforces the privacy rule: a user who was inactive for week 2 cannot read messages from that week even if they subscribe again in week 3.

Query params:
- `user_id` (required)
- `page`, `limit` (optional, default 1/20)

Sends a message to the group chat. The sender must:
1. Exist in the users table (401 if not)
2. Be an active group_member (403 if not)
3. Have a non-expired subscription_end_date (403 if expired)

Supports optional `replyTo` field (message ID prefixed with `gmsg_`).
A user_id that does not exist in the database must return 401 'User not found' before any group membership check is attempted.

exec: // Prerequisite: set subscription_end_date to a past date for this user+group in the DB

request.description: A user whose subscription_end_date has passed must receive 403. This enforces read-only mode for EXPIRED members — they can still fetch historical messages (GET) but cannot write new ones.

item.description: Read and write group chat messages. Messages are filtered to only show windows the user was actively subscribed for (discontinuous history support). Writing is blocked for expired or non-member users.

item.name: 4 · 1-on-1 Chat Sessions — Booking

request.description:
Returns the therapist's available chat slots for the next 14 days. Only slots with `is_available=true` and `type='chat'` can be booked via POST /chats/book.

Key response fields per slot:
- `slot_id` — pass this to the book endpoint
- `date`, `time` — scheduled window
- `price_ugx` — cost deducted from wallet at booking
- `is_available` — false if already booked or in the past

item.name: POST /chats/book — Book a Chat Session (Happy Path)

request.description:
Books a 1-on-1 chat session with a therapist. Deducts `price_ugx` from the client's wallet, creates a row in `chat_sessions` with status PENDING (displayed as LOCKED to clients), and marks the therapist slot as unavailable.

The response `status: 'LOCKED'` means the session is confirmed but not yet active — it transitions to ACTIVE at `scheduled_start_time`.

Double-booking prevention: if the therapist already has a chat_session overlapping the requested window, the endpoint returns 409 Conflict.

The test script auto-saves the returned `chat_id` to the `chatId` collection variable for downstream tests.

exec: // Run the happy-path book request first so this slot is already taken

request.description: Attempting to book the same therapist for an overlapping time window must return 409 Conflict. The overlap check queries chat_sessions for any existing row where `scheduled_start_time < requested_end AND scheduled_end_time > requested_start`.

item.description: Endpoints for browsing a therapist's available chat slots and booking a paid 1-on-1 session. A booked session starts in LOCKED (PENDING in DB) state and transitions to ACTIVE at the scheduled start time.

item.name: 5 · 1-on-1 Chat Sessions — Messaging
GET /chats/:chatId/messages — Fetch Messages with Session State

request.description:
Returns paginated messages for the chat plus the current session metadata. The `chat_status` field in the response reflects the client-facing state:
- `LOCKED` — session booked but start time not yet reached
- `ACTIVE` — session is live, both parties can send messages
- `CLOSED` — session ended, read-only

Also returns `is_read_only`, `scheduled_start_time`, `scheduled_end_time`.

Sends a message in a 1-on-1 chat. The session must be ACTIVE (i.e., current time is between scheduled_start_time and scheduled_end_time). The endpoint auto-checks the time window and will auto-close the session and return 403 if the end time has passed.

exec: // Prerequisite: update chat_sessions SET status='CLOSED' for this chatId in the DB

request.description: Sending a message to a CLOSED session must return 403. A session becomes CLOSED either when the scheduled_end_time passes (auto-closed on next heartbeat or send attempt) or when manually closed.

item.description: Send and receive messages within a booked chat session. Writing is gated on session status: LOCKED (PENDING) and CLOSED sessions reject new messages. An ACTIVE session auto-closes when the scheduled_end_time passes.

item.name: 6 · 1-on-1 Chat Sessions — Presence (Heartbeat)

request.description:
Updates the caller's `last_seen_at` timestamp in chat_sessions and returns:
- `is_peer_online` — true if the other party's last_seen_at is within 15 seconds
- `chat_status` — current state (LOCKED | ACTIVE | CLOSED)

State transitions handled automatically:
- PENDING → ACTIVE when `scheduled_start_time` is reached
- ACTIVE → CLOSED when `scheduled_end_time` is passed

The `user_id` in the body determines which party (client or therapist) is pinging.
A chatId that does not match any row in chat_sessions must return 400. This prevents silent failures when the app sends a heartbeat with a stale or malformed chat ID.

item.description: Short-poll heartbeat endpoint. Called every ~10 seconds by the client app to update last_seen_at and check if the peer is online. Also drives automatic session state transitions (PENDING→ACTIVE, ACTIVE→CLOSED).

item.name: 7 · Donations

request.description:
Deducts `amount` UGX from the user's wallet and records a credit entry in business_ledger with `source='donations'`.

Body params:
- `user_id` (required) — must exist in the users table
- `amount` (required) — must be > 0
- `note` (optional) — short message, max 255 chars, stored for reference

Response includes the transaction `reference`, new `wallet_balance`, and the `note` if provided.
A non-existent user_id must return 401 'User not found' before any balance check is performed.

When the requested donation amount exceeds the user's current wallet balance, the endpoint returns 400 with error code INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE and a breakdown showing `currentBalance`, `required`, and `shortfall` so the client can prompt the user to top up the exact shortfall.

item.description: Allows a client to make a one-time donation directly from their wallet. The amount is deducted from users.balance and credited to business_ledger with source='donations'.

item.name: 8 · Banners

exec: pm.test("imageUrl is null or full URL", () => {

request.description: Returns all active banners ordered by sort_order. Optional ?type filter (home | promo | alert).

exec: pm.test("All returned banners are promo type", () => {

request.description: Filters banners by type. Valid values: home, promo, alert.
Creates a new banner via JSON body. Use the multipart request below when uploading an image file.

exec: pm.test("imageUrl is full URL when file provided", () => {

request.description: Creates a banner with an image file upload. Uses multipart/form-data. The file field must be named banner_image or image.
title is required. Omitting it must return 400.

item.description: Dashboard banner management. GET retrieves active banners; POST creates a new banner (supports multipart file upload). Requires the banners table from migration_banners.sql.

item.name: 9 · Therapist Slots

request.description: Returns all slots for the given therapist. Filter by ?status=PENDING|ACTIVE|CLOSED is not applicable here — availability is 0 or 1.
Creates a new available slot for the therapist. Prevents duplicate date+time combinations.
Re-sending the same date + time for the same therapist must return 409.
Updates date, time, and/or availability of an existing slot. Ownership check: therapist_id must match the slot's therapist_id.
Attempting to edit a slot that belongs to a different therapist must return 404.
Permanently removes a slot. Ownership check applies.

item.description: Full CRUD for therapist time slots. Therapists manage their own availability — add, edit, or delete slots. Clients read slots via GET /api/v1/client/therapists/:id/slots.

item.name: 10 · Notifications

request.description: Returns paginated unread notifications for the user. Each item includes both body and message fields (same value, both map to the message DB column).
Marks a single notification as read. user_id is now required (was defaulting to 1 before, causing silent 404 failures).
Omitting user_id must now return 400 (previously silently failed with a 404 for the wrong user).
Marks all unread notifications as read for the given user.

item.description: Client notification endpoints. Fixed in this session: mark_read no longer silently fails when user_id is missing. Both GET endpoints now include a body field alongside message.

item.name: 11 · Therapist Session Start

request.description: Lists all 1-on-1 chat sessions for the therapist. Optional ?status=PENDING|ACTIVE|CLOSED filter.
Returns only sessions with PENDING (LOCKED) status — these are the sessions a therapist can start.
Transitions a PENDING (LOCKED) 1-on-1 session to ACTIVE. Sends a push notification to the client. Returns 200 no-op if already ACTIVE; 400 if CLOSED.

exec: // Prerequisite: set chat_sessions.status='CLOSED' for this session in DB

request.description: Attempting to start a CLOSED session must return 400.

Activates the group's current cohort (OPEN_FOR_BOOKING → ACTIVE) and promotes all PENDING member subscriptions to ACTIVE. Sends in-app notifications to all members. cohort_id is optional — defaults to the latest open cohort.

item.description: Endpoints for a therapist to start a booked 1-on-1 session or a group session. Starting transitions PENDING → ACTIVE and sends an in-app notification to the client(s).

item.name: 12 · Therapist List & Session Types
GET /client/therapists — List with Session Types & Image

exec: pm.test("image is null or full URL", () => {

request.description:
Returns all available therapists. Each therapist includes:
- image: full URL (BASE_URL + path) or null
- sessionTypes: array from therapist_session_types → session_types

Save the first therapist's user_id into {{therapistId}} for downstream slot tests.

item.name: GET /client/therapists/:id/slots — Slots with Session Types & Image

exec: pm.test("image is null or full URL", () => {

request.description: Returns a single therapist's profile (including image full URL), sessionTypes, and available_slots.

item.description: Therapist listing and slot endpoints now include sessionTypes pulled from therapist_session_types → session_types. Used by the client to show selectable session type options (e.g. Video Therapy, Chat Messaging Session).

item.name: 13 · Admin — Tester Credit

request.description: Adds `amount` UGX to the balance of every user where interface = 'tester'. Returns a list of credited accounts with their new balance. Also writes a business_ledger entry per user with source='tester_credit'.
Amount must be a positive number. Zero or negative values return 400.

item.description: Credits a specified amount to all users whose interface = 'tester'. Useful for seeding wallet balances before running tests.

item.name: 14 · Profile Picture Upload

request.description:
Uploads a profile picture for the user. Accepts multipart/form-data with a file field named 'avatar' or 'file'. Saves the file to /uploads/avatars/ and updates users.image with the relative path.

Returns avatarUrl as a full URL e.g. http://localhost:8000/uploads/avatars/user_123_abc.jpg
Sending the request without a file must return 400 'No file uploaded'.

exec: pm.test("avatarUrl in data when file provided", () => {

request.description:
Updates profile fields and optionally uploads a new profile picture in the same request. Send as multipart/form-data to include a file, or JSON for text fields only.

Supported fields: firstName, lastName, phoneNumber, dateOfBirth, gender, bio, avatar (file).

item.description: Endpoints for uploading and updating a client's profile picture. Two routes support this: the dedicated avatar endpoint and the general profile update endpoint (which also accepts a file).

variable.description: API base URL. Switch to https://server.innersparkafrica.us for production.
Value for the x-api-key header. Loaded from RC_SECRET in .env.
Test client user ID. Must exist in the users table with a non-zero wallet balance.
Test therapist ID. Must have at least one available slot in the slots table.
Test support group ID. Must exist in the groups table with group_status='active'.
Composite chat ID derived from client and therapist IDs. Format: chat_<client_id>_<therapist_id>.
Date used when booking a 1-on-1 chat session (YYYY-MM-DD).
Time used when booking a 1-on-1 chat session (HH:MM, 24-hour format).
ID of a chat_sessions row (1-on-1 session). Set by the book-session test script.
ID of a therapist slot row in the slots table.
ID of a group_cohorts row. Optional — start-group-session defaults to latest open cohort.
ID of a notifications row to mark as read.
